The '''Rogue Assassin''' was an ex-[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Advanced Recon Commando/Legends|Advanced Recon Commando]] who began work as a [[Mercenary/Legends|hired gun]]. During an internal dispute between the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] and the [[Techno Union/Legends|Techno Union]], the Rogue Assassin used the opportunity to take out key [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|CIS]] officers. On [[Polis Massa/Legends|Polis Massa]], the Rogue Assassin fought through [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] forces to locate and destroy six [[IG-100 MagnaGuard/Legends|MagnaGuards]]. The Rogue Assassin was eventually hired by [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance]] forces to eliminate six [[Clone Commander/Legends|Clone Commanders]] stationed on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. Fighting his former clone comrades, the Rogue Assassin was able to [[Death/Legends|kill]] the commanders. By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the Rogue Assassin was hired to hunt down groups of Imperial deserters on [[Dagobah/Legends|Dagobah]] and [[Mygeeto/Legends|Mygeeto]].<ref name="SWB2" />

==Equipment==
[[File:Clone Jet Trooper.jpg|thumb|150px|left|Rogue assassin in Phase II armor]]
The Rogue Assassin wore [[Phase I clone trooper armor/Legends|Phase I]] [[Clone jet trooper/Legends|clone jet trooper]] [[Armor/Legends|armor]] adorned with [[Color/Legends|green]] markings, and attached to his armor was a jetpack. He was armed with a [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]] and [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]]. The Rogue Assassin was also equipped with [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonators]] and a [[Fusioncutter/Legends|fusion cutter]].<ref name="SWB2" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Rogue Assassin appeared in the [[Wikipedia:PlayStation Portable|PSP version]] of ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]''. The game's instruction manual establishes that the clone was a former ARC trooper.<ref name="SWB2" />

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]'' {{C|[[Wikipedia:PlayStation Portable|PSP]] only}} {{1st}}
